title: Residual after the non-worktree-phase carve-out: two concurrent WORKTREE phases can still cross-contaminate the shared-tree tree-diff guard — needs a fleet-lane-owned-paths registry
why: The immediate cycle-967 fix exempts .evolve/ deliverable-prefix writes for NON-worktree phases (scout/triage/audit/etc.) — correct because those phases cannot author a worktree-bound deliverable, so such a path is always orchestrator/other-lane activity. But it does NOT cover two concurrent WORKTREE phases (e.g., two builds) where lane A's legit main-tree write is charged to lane B: the tree-diff guard diffs the SHARED cr.req.ProjectRoot with a per-lane beforeDirty baseline, so any concurrent write is a false 'new' path. Fully robust attribution needs lane ownership.
how_to_apply: Fleet supervisor maintains a shared registry (e.g. .evolve/runs/<run>/lane-owned-paths.json, written under each lane's flock) of paths each active lane is currently writing / its workspace surface. The tree-diff guard excludes leaked paths owned by ANOTHER active lane. Alternatively/additionally: re-snapshot beforeDirty at check time minus the union of other lanes' dirty sets. TDD: two concurrent worktree phases, lane A writes .evolve/phases/X, lane B's guard must NOT abort.
evidence: cyclerun_review.go:301 dr.treeGuard.Check(cr.ctx, cr.req.ProjectRoot, dr.beforeDirty) — shared ProjectRoot; cycle-967 root cause (lane-970 concurrent write).
